title: HTML Email Design Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the specialized techniques for coding robust, cross-client HTML emails.

questions.question: Coding an HTML email is different from coding a modern web page because...?
options: Email clients have widely varying support for modern CSS features.
HTML emails are only for text content.
You can only use one single HTML tag per email.
There is no difference between them.

questions.question: Which HTML element is the 'gold standard' for creating layout structures in emails to ensure consistency?
options: <div>
<section>
<table>
<article>

questions.question: What is the best way to apply CSS styles for an HTML email?
options: In an external .css file linked via <link>.
Using internal <style> tags only.
Using inline styles directly on each HTML element.
Using JavaScript only.

questions.question: What is the 'Ghost Table' technique used for in email development?
options: To hide images from certain users.
To provide a fallback layout specifically for older versions of Microsoft Outlook using conditional comments.
To create a background effect that looks like smoke.
To track how many users opened the email.

questions.question: Which ARIA role should be added to layout tables in an email to improve accessibility for screen reader users?
options: role='grid'
role='presentation'
role='banner'
role='table'
